reason: Winchester = sealed rigid fixed-platter hard disk assembly (a disk stack/pack); not removable and not flexible.
CD-RW = rewritable (reusable) optical disc; CD/DVD are generic, RPM is not storage.
Stem is garbled, but 'data gathering' = data collection/capture, i.e. allowing data to be INPUT; 'store' describes storage, not gathering.

Copying software from distribution media onto hard disk is INSTALLATION; option E 'Escalation' is almost certainly an OCR corruption of 'Installation' (Download/Upload are network transfers, Configuration is post-install setup).
An open document lives in volatile working memory = RAM.
Cache is the fast, expensive, small level managed by hardware (main memory/disk are managed by OS).
Babbage's Analytical Engine 'store' used columns of geared counter wheels; punched cards were input/program, not the memory unit.
The motherboard is the main board hosting CPU, RAM, ROM/BIOS and expansion slots.
Cache memory sits between CPU and main memory to reduce average access latency.
Logical memory is divided into pages; physical memory into frames of the same size.
RAM is by name/definition random access memory (ROM/PROM are also randomly addressable, but the intended answer is RAM).
Non-volatile persistence after power-off is provided by secondary storage; RAM/primary storage is volatile.
Floppy, magnetic tape, hard disc and optical disc are all non-volatile ('permanent') storage media.
Main memory is directly accessed by and works with the CPU (RAM IS main memory, so A is circular).
SRAM = Static Random-Access Memory.
Stem is garbled ('super computers floppy disc'); no floppy format has 400-700 MB (standard 3.5in = 1.44 MB; 400-700 M looks like CD-ROM capacity), so 'None of these' is the only defensible option.
Built-in/permanent internal memory shipped with the machine (firmware/BIOS) is ROM.
Virtual memory backs RAM with disk space (page/swap file) so programs can exceed physical RAM.
USB = Universal Serial Bus.
Memory experiments have a study/presentation phase then a test (retrieval) phase where recall is elicited.
